meta.title: Apparent Lack of Nucleotide Sequence Homology Between the Satellite and Genome RNA Species of Tomato Black Ring Virus

abstractHtml: In hybridization experiments using complementary DNA copies, no homology was detected between RNA-3 and either of the genome RNA species of tomato black ring virus. It is estimated that sequences amounting to 160 nucleotide residues shared by RNA-3 and RNA-1, or to 120 nucleotide residues shared by RNA-3 and RNA-2 would have been detected. It is concluded that RNA-3 is a true satellite RNA.
